6.7.19 Question: 
 
 
6.7.19 Solution: 
 
We can use a hash table to check the balance for every business transaction: 
тЧПтАЛ Initialize an empty Hash Table, where: 
тЧЛтАЛ The key is the transactionID 
тЧЛтАЛ The value is the net balance 
тЧПтАЛ For each the entries of n in the business transactions: 
тЧЛтАЛ If the entry is a debit, add the amount to the balance associated with the 
transactionID in the hash table 
тЧЛтАЛ If the entry is a credit, subtract the amount from the balance associated with the 
transactionID in the hash table 
тЧПтАЛ After process all entries, check the net balance of each key in the hash table: 
тЧЛтАЛ If every value is 0, then the system is in balance. 
тЧЛтАЛ If any value is non-zero, then the specific transaction is out of balance. 
 
The running time is 
. Since we process each transaction once, and there are n entries, 
ЁЭСВ(ЁЭСЫ)
which need n hash table operation (insert, lookup, update). Each operation expected 
 time, 
ЁЭСВ(1)
so need

 time. So the total running

time is
